LanguageData.CL_Playground_Overlay_Popover_Body: This is a popover anchored to the button above.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Feedback_Tooltip_Icon_Body: Press Ctrl to focus this button.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Feedback_Tooltip_Label_Par: The stormwall approaches from the east. Bind down anything unsheltered.
Highstorms recur on a roughly weekly cadence in this region.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Tabs_Body_Padded: Default 1rem of inner padding lifts the body off the divider.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Tabs_Body_FullBleed: This tab opted out of padding via noPaddingFor and sits flush with the divider.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Tabs_Body_LargePadding: Larger 2rem padding gives the body more room to breathe.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Drawer_ContentBody: This drawer slides in from the edge of the screen. Click outside or press Escape to dismiss.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Category_Layout_Desc: Containers that arrange children in rows, columns, grids, and stacks.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Category_Typography_Desc: Text roles - headings, body copy, captions, monospace.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Category_Buttons_Desc: Buttons of every shape - primary actions, icons, links.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Category_Inputs_Desc: Text fields, toggles, sliders, dropdowns - anything the user edits.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Category_Feedback_Desc: Spinners, progress, sparklines - the UI telling the user what is happening.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Category_Navigation_Desc: Tabs, breadcrumbs, menus - moving between places.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Category_Overlay_Desc: Modals, drawers, popovers, tooltips - temporary surfaces over the main view.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Category_Data_Desc: Lists, tables, trees - surfaces for inspecting collections.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Category_Hooks_Desc: State, refs, memoization, focus, animation - the primitives under the primitives.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Category_Blocks_Desc: Feature-scoped compositions of primitives - the main menu, dialog suites, and other named surfaces. Read these to see primitives in real use.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-main-menu-root_What: Top-level layered composition for the redesigned main menu. Stacks blurred background, metadata strip, hero, dock row, and branding into a single LightweaveNode tree.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-main-menu-root_When: Use as the single entry point invoked by MainMenuRedesignPatch when the RedesignMainMenu setting flag is on. Do not call directly from other surfaces.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-title-hero_What: RimWorld GameTitle texture rendered above a Caption-styled credit line.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-title-hero_When: Use at the top of the redesigned main menu hero column.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-continue-card_What: Wide hero card for the most recent save: map preview thumbnail, colony stats grid, amber Resume button.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-continue-card_When: Use in the center column of the redesigned main menu when a recent save is present. Composes HeroCard + Eyebrow + KeyValueTable + Button.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-expansion-row_What: Horizontal row of installed-expansion pills - Selected for active, Default for installed-but-disabled, Muted for not-installed.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-expansion-row_When: Use beneath TitleHero on the main menu. Returns null when no expansions are installed.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-metadata-table_What: Top-left metadata strip: build version, mods loaded, expansions, last save timestamp.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-metadata-table_When: Use in the corner of the main menu hero. Composes Eyebrow + KeyValueTable rows pulled live from VersionControl, ModsConfig, and the most recent save file.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-menu-buttons_What: Full main-menu dock row: New / Load / Mods / Tutorial / Options DockTiles plus the trailing MoreButton.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-menu-buttons_When: Use at the bottom of the redesigned main menu. Wraps DockTile + MoreButton in an HStack with proper spacing.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-dock-tile_What: Single dock entry: stacked icon + label with a hotkey badge in the corner. Custom paint - tile-shaped surface, hover lift.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-dock-tile_When: Use as one entry in the dock row. Reach for Button when you want a generic primary/secondary action instead.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-more-button_What: Sixth dock tile that opens the MorePopover. Tracks its rect via Hooks state to anchor the popover.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-more-button_When: Use at the right end of the dock row alongside MenuButtons.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-more-popover_What: Right-anchored popover panel of secondary main-menu actions: Credits, Translation report, Quit. Composes MenuItem primitives - Quit uses Danger styling.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-more-popover_When: Use as the popover content for MoreButton.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-accent-stripe_What: Slim accent-colored stripe used as a vertical or horizontal divider with shard branding. Pulls accent color from the active theme.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-accent-stripe_When: Use for edge accents on the main menu and other Cosmere-branded surfaces.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_block-lightweave-wordmark_: The 'POWERED BY LIGHTWEAVE' wordmark with accent dot. Pure typography composition - Eyebrow + dot.
Use in the footer of any Lightweave-driven surface that wants to attribute the toolkit.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Category_Tokens_Desc: Design tokens - breakpoints, spacing scale, theme slots. The vocabulary other primitives compose from.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_stack_What: Natural-height vertical stack. Each child gets its declared height; the stack exposes the total.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_stack_When: Use when you need scrollable content made of variable-height panels. Wrap in ScrollArea.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_card_What: Filled rectangular container with padding, background, and border.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_card_When: Group related controls. Default surface for a labeled section or demo cell.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_heading_What: Typographic heading at levels 1-4. Level controls size and weight.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_heading_When: Title a section. Match the level to the section's depth, not its importance.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_button_What: Primary interactive trigger. Fires a callback on click.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_button_When: Use for discrete actions. Reach for ButtonIcon for icon-only actions in tight space.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_textfield_What: Single-line text entry. Fires onSubmit on Enter and onBlur on focus loss.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_textfield_When: Collect a short string. Reach for SearchField when you want live filtering on keystroke.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_spinner_What: Rotating arc that indicates an ongoing operation.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_spinner_When: Use for operations taking longer than about half a second. Shorter than that, just update the UI.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_tabs_What: Horizontal row of tabs selecting which of several panels is visible.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_tabs_When: Use to split a surface into peer views. Prefer tabs over stacked modal dialogs.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_list_What: Virtualized scrollable list of rows rendered by a per-item callback.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_list_When: Use for long collections. Reach for Table when rows share a columnar structure.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_usestate_What: Per-render state hook. Returns a handle with a Value getter and Set method.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_usestate_When: Use whenever a primitive needs to remember something across renders.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Header_Subtitle: Every primitive, every theme, every direction.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Panel_SourceTooltip: Open this primitive's source file in VS Code.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Header_ForceDisabled_Toolt: Renders every interactive primitive in its disabled state. Useful for verifying disabled visuals without wiring per-demo state.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Panel_GithubTooltip: Open this primitive's source file on GitHub in your browser.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_column_What: Divides a rect evenly among its children, stacked top to bottom.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_column_When: Reach for it when you want equal-height rows. Use Stack when children have distinct heights.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_row_What: Divides a rect evenly among its children, laid out left to right. Reverses in RTL.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_row_When: Use for equal-width cells. Use HStack when some children need fixed widths and others flex.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_hstack_What: Horizontal layout with a mix of fixed-width and flex children.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_hstack_When: Use when one child should fill the remaining space. Reach for Row when every cell is equal.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_grid_What: Column-defined grid. Tracks can be fixed sizes, fractional weights, or repeated.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_grid_When: Use for tabular data or fixed column layouts. Use Wrap when items flow naturally without strict columns.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_wrap_What: Lays out children in rows, wrapping to the next row when width runs out.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_wrap_When: Use for tag clouds, chip lists, or any collection of uniform items that should reflow.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_scrollarea_What: Wraps content taller than its rect in a vertical scroll view.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_scrollarea_When: Use when content height exceeds the available space. Wrap a Stack to scroll the whole body.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_divider_What: A thin horizontal or vertical rule in the theme's subtle border color.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_divider_When: Use between logical sections that share a surface. Skip it when a gap already separates them.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_spacer_What: Invisible element reserving space. Flex spacers push adjacent content to the edges.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_spacer_When: Use to align content to the far edge of a container without hardcoded margins.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_each_What: Maps a collection to a list of nodes. Assigns stable keys for diffing across renders.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_each_When: Use to render one node per item in a sequence. Pass a keyFn when items can reorder.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_conditional_What: Wraps a child node so it is only built and painted when the predicate is true. When false, the entire subtree is skipped — no measure, no paint, no allocations. Compare with `Visibility`, which keeps the child mounted but hides it.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_conditional_When: Use to gate expensive subtrees behind a runtime flag (e.g., advanced settings, debug overlays, feature toggles). For trivial inline branches, a plain if-statement is fine.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_box_What: Low-level container with padding, background, border, and radius.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_box_When: Use when you need a styled rectangle. Reach for Card when you want the standard raised surface.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_vignette_What: Decorative overlay that darkens corners or edges to focus the eye.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_vignette_When: Frame a hero panel, dim the edges of a card, or fade content along one edge.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_text_What: The typographic atom. Renders a string with the chosen font, size, color, and weight.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_text_When: Use when you need control over font and color. Prefer Heading, Label, or Caption when they fit.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_label_What: Secondary-colored text at body-small size, in the Label font role.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_label_When: Use for form field labels and inline annotations. Caption is smaller and dimmer.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_caption_What: Small muted text intended for helper copy and metadata.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_caption_When: Use for timestamps, counts, and byline text beneath a heading.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_richtext_What: Body text that honors Unity's rich-text tags for bold, italic, and color runs.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_richtext_When: Use when a passage needs inline emphasis. Plain Text is faster for static content.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_code_What: Monospace text at body-small size, rendered in the Mono font role.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_code_When: Use for inline identifiers, file paths, and keyboard shortcuts.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_icon_What: Tinted texture drawn at a given size. Optionally mirrors in RTL.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_icon_When: Use wherever you need a glyph. Pair with Text to form an iconographic row.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_iconbutton_What: Button rendered as an icon alone, without a text label.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_iconbutton_When: Use in tight spaces like toolbars. Always provide a tooltip, since the icon is the only cue.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_togglebutton_What: Button that remembers an on or off state. Paints as Primary when on, Ghost when off.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_togglebutton_When: Use when a setting is a single binary. Reach for Switch when the control sits in a form.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_checkbox_What: Labeled boolean input rendered as a box with a check mark when true.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_checkbox_When: Use for independent on or off settings. Radio is for one-of-many choices.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_switch_What: Labeled track-and-thumb toggle for a boolean state.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_switch_When: Use when a setting takes effect immediately. Checkbox suits settings confirmed on save.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_radio_What: Mutually exclusive option group. Selecting one clears the rest.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_radio_When: Use for small sets of peer choices. Reach for Dropdown once the list grows past five or six.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_slider_What: Drag a thumb along a track to pick a value. Supports min, max, step, and tick marks.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_slider_When: Use for approximate numeric selection. NumberField is better when the exact value matters.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_slider-readout_What: Slider paired with a right-aligned mono-font numeric readout. The track gets the full available width; the readout sits in its own column.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_slider-readout_When: Use in settings rows where the player needs to see the exact current value (volume, UI scale, framerate cap). Pair with a label column for the setting name.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_textarea_What: Multi-line text entry that grows with its content up to a row limit.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_textarea_When: Use for paragraph-length input. TextField is right for single-line strings.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_numberfield_What: Single-line numeric entry. Clamps to min and max, shakes on invalid parse.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_numberfield_When: Use when a specific number matters. Slider is better for approximate tuning.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_searchfield_What: TextField with a magnifying glyph and a clear button that appears when filled.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_searchfield_When: Use when typing should immediately filter a list. TextField is for freeform entry.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_dropdown_What: Picks one value from a list in a popover menu. Supports type-ahead and keyboard navigation.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_dropdown_When: Use when the choice set is too long for Radio but too fixed for free text.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_colorpicker_What: Grid of swatches for picking a color from a palette.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_colorpicker_When: Use when color is a curated choice. A free RGB picker belongs in a dialog, not inline.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_keybinding_What: Captures a key combination on click. Shows the bound chord as text.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_keybinding_When: Use for letting the player rebind a hotkey. Clicking again enters recording mode.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_progressbar_What: Horizontal bar showing progress from 0 to 1. Optional centered label.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_progressbar_When: Use for determinate tasks. Spinner is right when the duration is unknown.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_ringgauge_What: Circular gauge drawn as an arc fill. Supports a centered label.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_ringgauge_When: Use for at-a-glance readouts like Stormlight or investiture reserves.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_chart_What: Line chart with optional axes, gridlines, hover tooltip, and per-point highlight.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_chart_When: Use anywhere a metric over time matters. Inline as a sparkline, or full-size with axes for dashboards.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_badge_What: Pill-shaped label in a status color. Optional leading and trailing icons. Optionally clickable, with a separate trailing-click for dismiss-style affordances.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_badge_When: Use to mark state, category, or a filter chip. Pass `onTrailingClick` and `Badge.CloseGlyph` for dismissibles; pass `onClick` to make the whole pill actionable.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_pill_What: Tag-style chip with optional leading icon, label, and full-pill click. Three visual variants: default, selected, and muted.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_pill_When: Use for filter chips, expansion / faction / trait tags, mode toggles. Reach for `Badge` instead when you want a compact non-interactive status pip.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_tooltip_What: Wraps any node. Shows a floating popover after a short hover delay.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_tooltip_When: Use for secondary information. Put the essential label in the trigger itself.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_segmented_What: Pill group of peer choices, one active at a time. Like Radio for a toolbar.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_segmented_When: Use for two to five short labels that share a surface. Tabs are for whole views.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_breadcrumbs_What: Path of labels joined by chevrons. Truncates middle segments when space runs out.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_breadcrumbs_When: Use in deep hierarchies where the player needs a jump back to an ancestor.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_menu_What: Popover list of actions. Supports icons, disabled items, and nested submenus.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_menu_When: Use for four or more peer actions behind a trigger. Inline buttons win for two or three.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_menu-item_What: Standalone clickable menu-style row. Hover background, optional leading icon, optional danger styling for destructive actions.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_menu-item_When: Use when composing a custom popover, drawer panel, or sidebar that needs menu-item visuals without the full Menu widget. Reach for `Menu.Create` when you want keyboard nav and submenus.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_menu-divider_What: Standalone horizontal divider for use inside custom menu-style stacks. Pairs with MenuItem.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_menu-divider_When: Use between groups of related MenuItems when composing a custom popover, drawer, or sidebar.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_contextmenu_What: Wraps a region so right-click opens a menu at the mouse position.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_contextmenu_When: Use for per-item actions on a list row. Do not hide the primary action behind right-click.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_dialog_What: Floating window opened on top of the main view. Dims the background.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_dialog_When: Use for choices that must be made before proceeding. Avoid stacking dialogs.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_dialog-header_What: Top bar for redesigned dialogs. Composes optional eyebrow breadcrumb, display title, optional trailing text action, and optional close button.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_dialog-header_When: Use at the top of every Lightweave-styled dialog (Load Colony, Mods Config, Options) so titles, breadcrumbs, and chrome stay aligned across the suite.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_popover_What: Floating panel anchored to a trigger rect. Dismisses on outside click.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_popover_When: Use for light-weight menus and short pickers. Dialog is right for heavier interactions.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_drawer_What: Panel that slides in from an edge of the screen. Left, right, top, or bottom.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_drawer_When: Use for secondary views that should not interrupt the main surface. Dialog blocks, Drawer defers.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_toast_What: Transient notification in a screen corner. Fades on its own after a few seconds.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_toast_When: Use for background events the player should notice but not act on immediately.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_window_What: Standalone floating frame opened over the map. Border, resize behavior, and minimum size are all configurable.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_window_When: Use for tools and readouts the player leaves open while playing. Reach for Dialog when a choice must be resolved before proceeding.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Window_Sample_Bordered_Bod: Themed border: subtle stroke, surface fill, and rounded corners. Drag the top strip to move; hover any edge or corner to resize.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Window_Sample_Borderless_B: Border disabled. Surface fill only, no stroke or rounded corners. Drag the top strip to move; hover any edge or corner to resize.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Window_Sample_Fixed_Body: Edge resize disabled. The window opens at a fixed size and cannot be stretched. Useful for dialogs whose layout only reads at one size.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Window_Sample_Large_Body: Large initial size with a higher minimum. Try shrinking past the minimum; the edge clamps so the window never falls below its declared floor.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Window_Sample_Dialog_Body: WindowFooter pinned to the bottom hosts a leading-flex spacer plus Cancel and Confirm buttons. The body region naturally absorbs the remaining height.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Window_Sample_Status_Body: Footer hosts a status caption and a corner resize grip. Drag the grip to test edge-resize against the bottom-trailing corner.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_table_What: Virtualized grid of rows with a fixed header. Columns take fixed widths or fractional weights.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_table_When: Use when rows share a columnar structure. List suffices for freeform rows.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_tree_What: Virtualized list of expandable nodes. Children render indented under their parent.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_tree_When: Use for hierarchical data. Flatten to a List when depth rarely exceeds one.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_keyvalue_What: Two-column row with a fixed-width label on the start edge and a value node on the end.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_keyvalue_When: Use for inspector-style readouts. Stack many to form a property sheet.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_useanim_What: Interpolates a float toward a target value with a duration and easing function.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_useanim_When: Use to smooth transitions in painted values. Per-frame animation registers an active clock tick.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_usefocus_What: Produces a focus handle that can request or clear keyboard focus for its control.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_usefocus_When: Use when a button should move focus to a text field. Chain with TextField to focus on open.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_usehotkey_What: Registers a keyboard shortcut handler while this subtree is rendered.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_usehotkey_When: Use to bind Escape, Ctrl+S, and similar chords. The handler runs on each matching key press.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_card_Sectioned_Desc: Confirm the oath. A bond once spoken cannot be unsaid.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_card_Sectioned_Body: Speak the Words. The bond settles.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_card_Pawn_Note: Honor before all. Strength before weakness. Journey before destination.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_card_Loose_Desc: Bonds tighten with each Ideal. There is no return from the next.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_accordion_What: Stacked headers that reveal content when expanded. Single mode closes siblings; Multi mode lets any number stay open.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_accordion_When: Use for inspector sections, FAQ-style disclosure, or long forms broken into groups. Reach for Tabs when the sections are peer views.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_accordion_Body_Overview: Roshar endures the highstorms. Every living thing on the world has adapted to weather them, or to draw from the stormlight they leave behind.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_accordion_Body_Stormlight: Stormlight fills gemstones infused during a highstorm. It holds its own weight and can be drawn on by those bonded to spren to fuel their powers.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_accordion_Body_Spren: Spren are splinters of investiture given form by human thought. Bonded spren grant surges; wild spren gather around the emotions and acts they echo.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_sidenav_What: Vertical category rail with inline sub-nav that expands on click or after a short hover. Scrolls when content overflows.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_sidenav_When: Use as the primary navigator for surfaces with many peer views. Reach for Tabs when the set is short and horizontal space is free.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_buttongroup_What: Row of connected buttons sharing borders and corner rounding. Each button fires an independent action.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_buttongroup_When: Use for a small cluster of peer actions that belong together. Reach for Segmented when the buttons represent mutually-exclusive choices.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_carousel_What: Animated horizontal slide view. Arrow zones at the edges advance; dots below jump to a specific index.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_carousel_When: Use for onboarding walk-throughs, image galleries, or any ordered sequence the player scrubs through. Skip it when all content should be visible at once.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_container_What: Width-constrained wrapper that caps its child at a maximum width and centers it horizontally. Optional horizontal padding defines the gutter.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_container_When: Wrap long-form bodies and dashboards in a Container so their content stays readable on wide windows. Skip it for full-bleed surfaces like HUD panes.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_breakpoints_What: Tailwind-style viewport tokens (Xs, Sm, Md, Lg, Xl, Xxl) resolved from the painted root width. Read RenderContext.Current.Breakpoint or wrap a value in Responsive<T> so layout adapts when the window crosses a threshold.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_breakpoints_When: Reach for breakpoints when a single layout cannot serve narrow and wide windows well. Skip them for fixed surfaces like HUD overlays whose footprint never changes.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Hook_UseFocus_StatusIdle: Idle. Click the field to focus.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Hook_UseHotkey_ModifiersHi: Click here, then Ctrl+S or Ctrl+Z.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Hook_UseBreakpoint_Adaptiv: Wide window. Show two columns of detail.
Medium window. Single column with secondary actions.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_Hook_UseBreakpoint_PickRef: Two columns. Primary content beside the rail.
Three columns. Rail, primary, and a detail pane.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_rem_What: Sizing primitive used everywhere a length is needed. 1 Rem resolves to Spacing.BaseUnit pixels for layout (padding, gaps, borders) and Spacing.FontBaseUnit pixels for type. Adjusting either constant rescales the whole system uniformly.
LanguageData.CL_Playground_rem_When: Reach for Rem (or a SpacingScale step) anywhere a size is requested. Avoid raw float pixel literals so the design tokens stay the single source of truth.
